header.title: Priest in Carentan, 1944 2017 (36939141644).jpg
header.description:
105mm Howitzer Motor Carriage M7 in Carentan, 64 Holgate Street, 7th véhicule Baker Compagny, 14th Armored Field Artillery Battalion assigned 15 July 1940 to the 2nd Armored Division and activated at Fort Benning, Georgia.

Canon Automoteur M7 obusier de 105mm traversant le 64 Rue Holgate en 1944, le 7e de la compagnie Baker, 14e régiment d'artillerie, 2e Division Blindée
header.author: Falcon® Photography

header.title: Carentan 1944 2017 (34533183833).jpg
header.description:
Le 14 juin 1944, un convoi de Jeeps traverse la rue Holgate.

<a href="http://www.dday-overlord.com/bataille-normandie/communes/manche/carentan" rel="noreferrer nofollow">www.dday-overlord.com/bataille-normandie/communes/manche/...</a>
header.author: Falcon® Photography

header.title: Kubelwagen dans Carentan, 1944 2017 (35176506682).jpg
header.description:
Des paras américains traversent Carentan à bord d'une Kübelwagen capturée aux parachutistes allemands.

<a href="http://www.dday-overlord.com/bataille-normandie/communes/manche/carentan" rel="noreferrer nofollow">www.dday-overlord.com/bataille-normandie/communes/manche/...</a>
header.author: Falcon® Photography

photos.title: G-AMSN Nose art on C-47 Skytrain ‘292717’ “Stoy Hora” aircraft (WWII D-Day Experience and Dead Man's Corner Museum, Carentan les Marais, Normandy, France 2022-07-10 by Alan Wilson HawkeyeUK).jpg
photos.description:
Nose art on C-47 Skytrain ‘292717’ “Stoy Hora” (really 44-77047 / G-AMSN)
c/n 16631 / 33379
Built in 1945 and delivered to the US military in June that year as a C-47B with the serial 44-77047. Immediately transferred to the Royal Air Force as a Dakota IV with the British military serial KN673.
Sold off in 1952, it became G-AMSN on the British civil register and flew with Starways, Mercury Airways and South Coast Air Services. US civil registered as N3455 in December 1967, she ended up with Pyra
photos.author: HawkeyeUK

photos.title: G-AMSN Douglas Dakota aircraft (WWII D-Day Experience and Dead Man's Corner Museum, Carentan les Marais, Normandy, France 2022-07-10 by Alan Wilson HawkeyeUK).jpg
photos.description:
Douglas C-47B-35-DK Skytrain ‘292717 / 8Y-S’ “Stoy Hora” (really 44-77047 / G-AMSN)
c/n 16631 / 33379
Built in 1945 and delivered to the US military in June that year as a C-47B with the serial 44-77047. Immediately transferred to the Royal Air Force as a Dakota IV with the British military serial KN673.
Sold off in 1952, it became G-AMSN on the British civil register and flew with Starways, Mercury Airways and South Coast Air Services. US civil registered as N3455 in December 1967, she ended up
photos.author: HawkeyeUK

photos.title: Normandie Carentan DDay experience2eme Jour 1837 JMD GF. Normandy 1944 D-Day site, memorials, museums (Jean-Michel Dromard, 2024-08-24).jpg
photos.description:
Image from the Normandie D Day 2024 album by Jean-Michel Dromard, taken on 24 August 2024 – eighty years after the Normandy landings – at historic sites, memorial exhibitions, and museums in Normandy, including Arromanches-les-Bains, the coastal batteries and fortifications of Batterie de Longueville and Batterie de Merville (Merville Gun Battery), as well as the exhibitions at the D-Day Experience attraction in Carentan-les-Marais.
The Normandy landings were the amphibious and airborne operatio
photos.author: jean-micheldromard
